text.title: Azure Application Gateway v2 + WAF Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Azure Application Gateway v2 + WAF` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: App Gateway MUST live in a dedicated subnet ≥ /26 with delegation to `Microsoft.Network/applicationGateways`.
rule.rationale: Sharing the subnet or missing delegation fails Terraform apply at the API level.

rule.statement: WAF policy MUST start in Detection mode; switch to Prevention only after ≥ 1 week of false-positive review.
rule.rationale: Going straight to Prevention silently blocks legitimate traffic on SQL-shaped payloads.

rule.statement: WAF policy MUST use OWASP CRS 3.2 as the managed ruleset baseline. Bot protection ruleset MUST be enabled on internet-facing gateways.
rule.rationale: Older CRS versions miss 2025-era exploit patterns; bot protection prevents commodity scraping abuse.

rule.statement: TLS certs MUST be sourced via `key_vault_secret_id`; PFX upload to App Gateway is forbidden. Managed identity MUST have `get` + `list` cert permissions.
rule.rationale: PFX uploads drift on rotation; Key Vault integration syncs renewals automatically.

rule.statement: SSL policy MUST require ≥ TLS 1.2 (`AppGwSslPolicy20220101S` or stricter).
rule.rationale: TLS 1.0/1.1 fail PCI 4.0 + modern compliance regimes.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
